Paragraph.Text: direct that the goods are no longer to be detained for the purpose of the proceedings, on any conditions that the court may impose, if their owner, importer, exporter or consignee furnishes security in an amount fixed by the court.

Subsection.Text: If a party applies to have the detained goods stored in a place other than a bonded warehouse or a sufferance warehouse, as those terms are defined in subsection 2(1) of the Customs Act, the Minister must consent to the storage of the goods in that place before a condition to that effect is imposed under subsection (1).
The court may impose a condition described in subsection (2) despite section 31 of the Customs Act.

A direction under paragraph (1)(b) that the goods are no longer to be detained for the purpose of the proceedings does not preclude a customs officer from continuing to detain the goods under the Customs Act for another purpose.

In the course of proceedings referred to in subsection 51.06(3), the court may, on the application of the Minister or a party to the proceedings, require the owner of the relevant registered trade-mark to furnish security, in an amount fixed by the court,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: to cover duties, as defined in subsection 2(1) of the Customs Act, storage and handling charges, and any other amount that may become chargeable against the goods; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: to answer any damages that may by reason of the detention be sustained by the owner, importer, exporter or consignee of the goods.

Section.Text: A court may award damages against the owner of a relevant registered trade-mark who commenced proceedings referred to in subsection 51.06(3) to the owner, importer, exporter or consignee of the goods who is a party to the proceedings for losses, costs or prejudice suffered as a result of the detention of goods if the proceedings are dismissed or discontinued.
The definition release in section 52 of the Act is repealed.
Sections 53.2 and 53.3 of the Act are replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Power of court to grant relief

Subsection.Text: If a court is satisfied, on application of any interested person, that any act has been done contrary to this Act, the court may make any order that it considers appropriate in the circumstances, including an order providing for relief by way of injunction and the recovery of damages or profits, for punitive damages and for the destruction or other disposition of any offending goods, packaging, labels and advertising material and of any equipment used to produce the goods, packaging, labels or advertising material.

Before making an order for destruction or other disposition, the court shall direct that notice be given to any person who has an interest or right in the item to be destroyed or otherwise disposed of, unless the court is of the opinion that the interests of justice do not require that notice be given.

Section.MarginalNote: Unaltered state — exportation, sale or distribution

Subsection.Text: A court is not permitted, in any proceeding under section 53.1 or 53.2, to make an order under that section requiring or permitting the goods to be exported, sold or distributed in an unaltered state, except in a manner that does not affect the legitimate interests of the owner of the registered trade-mark or except in exceptional circumstances, if the court finds that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: goods bearing the registered trade-mark have been imported into Canada in such a manner that the distribution of the goods in Canada would be contrary to this Act; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the registered trade-mark has, without the consent of the owner, been applied to those goods with the intent of counterfeiting or imitating the trade-mark, or of deceiving the public and inducing them to believe that the goods were made with the consent of the owner.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) also applies with respect to goods for which the only alteration is the removal of the trade-mark.

Section.Text: Section 55 of the Act is replaced by the following:

The Federal Court has jurisdiction to entertain any action or proceeding, other than a proceeding under section 51.01, for the enforcement of any of the provisions of this Act or of any right or remedy conferred or defined by this Act.
Subsection 57(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Federal Court has exclusive original jurisdiction, on the application of the Registrar or of any person interested, to order that any entry in the register be struck out or amended on the ground that at the date of the application the entry as it appears on the register does not accurately express or define the existing rights of the person appearing to be the registered owner of the trade-mark.

Section.Text: Section 60 of the Act is replaced by the following:

When any appeal or application has been made to the Federal Court under any of the provisions of this Act, the Registrar shall, at the request of any of the parties to the proceedings and on the payment of the prescribed fee, transmit to the Court all documents on file in the Registrar’s office relating to the matters in question in those proceedings, or copies of those documents certified by the Registrar.
Section 64 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Subject to the regulations, any document, information or fee that is provided to the Registrar under this Act may be provided in any electronic form, and by any electronic means, that is specified by the Registrar.
Subject to the regulations, the Registrar may use electronic means to create, collect, receive, store, transfer, distribute, publish, certify or otherwise deal with documents or information.
In this section, electronic, in reference to a form or means, includes optical, magnetic and other similar forms or means.
Paragraphs 65(a) and (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the form of the register to be kept under this Act, and of the entries to be made in it;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: applications to the Registrar;

Paragraph.Label: (b.1)
Paragraph.Text: the merger of registrations under paragraph 41(1)(f), including, for the purpose of renewal under section 46, the deemed day of registration or last renewal;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 65(d) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: certificates of registration;

Subsection.Text: Section 65 of the Act is amended by striking out “and” at the end of paragraph (d.1), by adding “and” at the end of paragraph (e) and by adding the following after paragraph (e):

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: the provision of documents, information or fees to the Registrar under this Act, including the time at which they are deemed to be received by the Registrar.

Section.Text: Section 69 of the Act and the heading before it are replaced by the following:

The disclosure of documents — on which entries in the register to be kept under paragraph 26(1)(b), as it read immediately before the day on which subsection 27(1) of the Combating Counterfeit Products Act comes into force, are based — is subject to subsection 50(6), as it read on June 8, 1993.
Section 30.1 does not apply with respect to an application for registration whose filing date is before the day on which section 30 of the Combating Counterfeit Products Act comes into force.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 33(1), as enacted by section 32 of the Combating Counterfeit Products Act, does not apply to an application for registration with respect to which all of the items set out in that subsection 33(1) have been received by the Registrar before the day on which that section 32 comes into force.

Section 25 of the Trade-marks Regulations, as it read immediately before the day on which section 32 of Combating Counterfeit Products Act comes into force, continues to apply to an application for registration referred to in subsection (1) that does not have a filing date as of that day.

Section.Text: Section 34, as it read immediately before the day on which subsection 33(1) of the Combating Counterfeit Products Act comes into force, continues to apply with respect to an application for registration that has a filing date, established without taking section 34 into account, that is before that day.
Section 69 of the Act is repealed.
The Act is amended by replacing “wares”, wherever it occurs, with “goods”.
The French version of the Act is amended by replacing “colis” with “emballages”, with any grammatical changes that the circumstances require, in the following provisions:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: subsections 4(1) and (3); and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: section 8.

Section.Text: The English version of the Act is amended by replacing “date of filing” with “filing date” in the following provisions:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the portion of subsection 16(2) before paragraph (a); and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 21(1).

Section.Text: The French version of the Act is amended by replacing “utiliser”, “utilisé”, “utilisée” and “utilisation” with “employer”, “employé”, “employée” and “emploi”, respectively, with any grammatical changes that the circumstances require, in the following provisions:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the portion of paragraph 7(d) before subparagraph (i);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: paragraph 9(1)(f);

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: section 11.1;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the portion of subsection 11.14(2) before paragraph (a);

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the portion of subsection 11.15(2) before paragraph (a);

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: subsections 11.16(1) and (2);

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: the portion of subsection 11.17(1) before paragraph (a);

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: subsections 11.18(1) to (4);

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: the portion of section 11.2 before paragraph (a);

Paragraph.Label: (j)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 17(2);

Paragraph.Label: (k)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 20(2);

Paragraph.Label: (l)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 32(2).

Section.Text: The Registrar of Trade-marks may amend the register kept under section 26 of the Trade-marks Act to reflect the amendments to that Act that are made by this Act.
Schedule II to the Access to Information Act is amended by striking out the reference to

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Loi sur les marques de commerce

Subsection.Text: The definition offence in section 183 of the Criminal Code is amended by adding the following after paragraph (d):

Paragraph.Label: (d.1)
Paragraph.Text: section 42 (offences related to infringement of copyright) of the Copyright Act,

Subsection.Text: The definition offence in section 183 of the Act is amended by striking out “or” at the end of subparagraph (i)(vi), by adding “or” at the end of paragraph (j) and by adding the following after paragraph (j):

Paragraph.Label: (k)
Paragraph.Text: section 51.01 (offences related to goods, labels, packaging or services) of the Trade-marks Act,

Section.Text: Subsection 107(5) of the Customs Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (l):

Paragraph.Label: (l.1)
Paragraph.Text: any person who may receive information under section 44.03 or subsection 44.04(1) of the Copyright Act, solely for the purposes for which that person is entitled to the information;

Paragraph.Label: (l.2)
Paragraph.Text: any person who may receive information under section 51.05 or subsection 51.06(1) of the Trade-marks Act, solely for the purposes for which that person is entitled to the information;

Section.Text: Paragraphs 5(1)(a) and (b) of the Olympic and Paralympic Marks Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: of any offending goods, packaging, labels and advertising material; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: of any equipment used to apply to those goods, packaging, labels or advertising material a mark whose adoption or use is prohibited under section 3.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by replacing “wares”, wherever it occurs, with “goods”.

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsections (2) and (3), the provisions of this Act — other than sections 1, 3 and 4, subsections 7(1) and (4), sections 10, 11 and 14, subsection 15(2), sections 19, 22, 25 and 26, subsection 37(2) and sections 42, 45, 46, 53, 54, 56, 59, 61 and 62 — come into force on a day or days to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.
Sections 2, 5 and 6, subsection 7(6) and sections 43, 44 and 60 come into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.
Sections 52 and 58 come into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.
